category_title: Quietly observant students who listen to stories or watch peers solve math problems
minimal_context: The student is sitting on a small wooden chair on the carpet area to listen to a teacher read a story about the water cycle.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to sit on the carpet and listen to a story about the water cycle.

STUDENT: (Selects a fidget item prior to sitting on the small wooden chair on the carpet surrounded by peers.)

TUTOR: I am going to start reading the book now.

STUDENT: (Sits quietly, watching other students instead of looking at the book.)

TUTOR: Please pay attention to the story.

STUDENT: (Remains passively engaged during the whole-group read-aloud, just staring straight ahead and watching peers.)

options.A: Prompt the student to select from a variety of fidget items prior to sitting on the carpet.
options.B: Use a 'request for clarification', asking the student for explanations and elaborations about the story.
options.C: Read the book slowly, using both English and Korean, adding some Korean explanations to the author’s writing.
options.D: Nod or try to maintain eye contact with the student for as long as possible during the read-aloud.
